message: You're right about that too, Snipes. Since this boy is here, I guess you’ve heard the news about the talent, Cindy.
In this already cramped room, there are five people, all clustered around my desk. It's so cramped it's unbearable.
With the appearance of the detectives,
the boy seems to be getting even more timid. He's ready to flee at the slightest chance.
he's out of sight. Is he scared or excited to see real detectives?
He's still that carefree old detective. Despite his constant smile, he's surely thinking of how to trap me.
He's positioned himself so he can observe both me and Detective Maurice at the same time. He's not just an old man after all.
Even though it's not the first time seeing him, I can’t help but feel an indescribable intimidation. I guess it's the wisdom that comes with age.
Standing behind, he's making sure he can catch me if I try to flee.
Using his towering height of nearly seven feet to intimidate us. Unfortunately,
I’m no match for someone like that.
He's still the same condescending guy as ever. Quite different from Detective Maurice, who is humble for his age.
I never thought I'd have to come back again.
You really seem to be connected to this case, don't you?
Still, what's strange is you. Why are you acquainted with two women who were brutally murdered in this town?
Hey, didn't you do the killing yourself?
That's right, you shouldn't say such rude things.
Are you planning to visit Meryl too?
I think I'll have to speak with those involved.
She still doesn't know that her daughter was Cindy.
What's up, haven't you told her?
Got it, I'll talk to her keeping that in mind.
Excuse me for bothering you this morning, Mr. Snipes.
I didn’t think I'd be bothering you this many times.
You're a rude person, aren't you?
Make sure you listen carefully to the old man’s story.
Is it true that Cindy is dead?
Unfortunately. My son was a fan of hers.
I heard that an ankle was sent to her house?
You heard it from me. Apparently, the right ankle was the first to arrive.
We received word that the ankle arrived, and secretly sent out a search team for Cindy.
Sadly, she was found among the garbage. Such a tragic end for someone who was so popular.
It was found at the Greenwich Village dump.
I hear she also came here. If you know anything, I’d like to hear about it.
You seemed to be close with Cindy, was something going on?
Well, between the two of them—
She was someone involved with my work.
More accurately, she used to be.
You probably have your client’s interests in mind, but I’d like to know if possible.
A woman came to me asking to find her daughter she put up for adoption. That daughter is Cindy, also known as Ariana.
What, Cindy had such a past!?
I'd like to hear from her mother.
Promise me you won't leak it to the press?
Actress Meryl is her mother, huh?
What happened to the Eva Courtney case after that?
That's something you don't need to know, right? Or are you worried about the investigation because you did the killing?
That's a rude way to put it.
As of now, there’s no suspect, Mr. Snipes.
I heard it’s the same perpetrator as the serial murder case.
it's definitely plausible. But where did you hear that?
I think it's a stretch to assume Eva Courtney was killed by the serial murder case perpetrator.
She was dismembered completely. Sure, dismemberment is a commonality.
But being completely dismembered is clearly irregular.
Don't worry; the police are doing their utmost to catch the culprit.
When we had Cindy’s body confirmed, she just stayed silent without saying anything. It must have really hit her hard.
She was very important to her.
I thought she might be the culprit, but it seems that’s not the case.
There’s no way Gloria could be the culprit.
You’re an idiot, you never know what a jealous woman might do.
How did you come to know them?
I may have mentioned it before, but I was invited to Director Fabar's home party.
Why would you go to something like that?
I was invited as Guy's friend.
I might ask you more about it later.
